# agentops_channel (Resource)

A notification channel that delivers agent events to an external destination (e.g. Slack). Routes are managed with `agentops_channel_route`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "agentops_channel" "alerts" {
  channel_provider = "slack"
  connector        = "bot"
  display_name     = "prod-alerts"

  # app_token is write-only and never returned by the API.
  app_token = var.slack_bot_token

  config = jsonencode({
    default_channel = "C0123456789"
  })

  labels = {
    env = "production"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `channel_provider` (String) Channel provider (e.g. `slack`). Named `channel_provider` because `provider` is a reserved Terraform argument. Changing this forces a new channel.
- `connector` (String) Connector kind within the provider. Changing this forces a new channel.
- `display_name` (String) Human-readable channel name.

### Optional

- `app_token` (String, Sensitive) Provider app/bot token. Write-only; never returned by the API.
- `config` (String) Provider-specific configuration, as a JSON object.
- `external_id` (String) External identifier for the channel in the provider. Changing this forces a new channel.
- `integration_id` (String) ID of the integration connection backing this channel. Changing this forces a new channel.
- `labels` (Map of String) Arbitrary key/value labels applied to the channel.
- `status` (String) Delivery status. Set to `paused` to suspend delivery or `active` to resume it.

### Read-Only

- `account_id` (String) Account that owns the channel.
- `capabilities_json` (String) Provider-reported capabilities, as a JSON object.
- `created_at` (String) Creation timestamp.
- `created_by` (String) Principal that created the channel.
- `id` (String) Channel identifier.
- `last_event_at` (String) Timestamp of the last event delivered through the channel.
- `slug` (String) URL-safe channel slug.
- `updated_at` (String) Last-update timestamp.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Channels are imported by their id.
terraform import agentops_channel.alerts chn_01hxyz
```
